A service that is ordinarily considered unskilled could be considered a skilled therapy 
service in cases where there is clear documentation that, because of special medical 
complications, skilled rehabilitation personnel are required to perform the service. 
However, the importance of a particular service to a patient or the frequency with which 
it must be performed does not, by itself, make an unskilled service into a skilled service.
